---
title: "Econometrics Typst Extension Demo"
author: "Giuseppe Ragusa"
date: "2026"
format:
  econometrics-typst-typst:
    number-sections: true
    papersize: a4
    mainfont: "Latin Modern Roman"
    mathfont: "Latin Modern Math"
    titlepage: false
---

# Probability Review

## Expectations and Variance

Consider a random variable $X$ with support on $\R$. The population is $\calP$.

The expected value is $\E*{X}$ and the variance is $\var*{X} = \E*{(X - \E*{X})^2}$.

The conditional expectation $\CE*{Y \given X}$ is the best predictor of $Y$ given $X$. The law of iterated expectations states
$$
\E*{Y} = \E*{\CE*{Y \given X}}
$$ {#eq-lie}

From @eq-lie we see that the unconditional mean averages the conditional mean.

## Vectors and Matrices

The design matrix is $\mX$ with rows $\vx_i$. The coefficient vector is $\vbeta$, error covariance is $\mSigma$, and the projection coefficient is $\vdelta$.

## Convergence

For iid data $X_i \simiid (\mu, \sigma^2)$, we have $\bar{X}_n \pto \mu$ and $\sqrt{n}(\bar{X}_n - \mu) \dto \Normal(0, \sigma^2)$.

If $X \indep Y$ then $\cov*{X, Y} = 0$.

## Delimiters

Parentheses $\paren*{\frac{a}{b}}$, brackets $\brock*{x}$, norm $\norm*{\vx}$, absolute value $\abs*{x}$, ceiling $\ceil*{x}$, floor $\floor*{x}$.

::: {#definition-cef}
## Conditional Expectation Function

The CEF of $Y$ given $X = x$ is $\mu(x) = \CE*{Y \given X = x}$.
:::

::: {#theorem-wlln}
## Weak Law of Large Numbers

If $\E*{\abs*{X}} < \infty$, then $\bar{X}_n = \frac{1}{n}\sumin X_i \pto \E*{X}$.
:::

::: {#remark-wlln-note}
@theorem-wlln requires only a finite first moment.
:::

::: {.ananote}
The linear projection $\LP*{Y \given 1, X}$ and the CEF $\CE*{Y \given X}$ coincide when the CEF is linear.
:::
